occupation: Chief Product Architect at Media Resources Inc.
summary: Modern-day generalist with a very strong technical (engineering) background and aptitude for developing new skills. Deeply passionate about the process of innovation and creation: taking an abstract and novel idea and working it until a real product can be delivered to a customer. Revels in solving challenging problems with cross-disciplinary approaches but unfettered by any need to dive deep in specialized areas. Leads with confidence, empathy and loyalty, with a track record of building great technical teams. Well proven in all of the above.

experiences.title: Senior Engineering Product Manager
experiences.description: Product Management and Development - Overall lead product manager for our 80% revenue product line VISIONiQ, defining product vision, competitive analysis, market positioning, and engineering deliverables. - One of three engineering team leaders for VISIONiQ, specialized in optics, data-distribution and controls networks, base-component manufacturing, and electrical systems. - Over 2.5 years, created product growth from an unknown product and brand, through 1.5 generations of major product overhauls, to becoming a preferred product to top tier customers in DOOH and sign manufacturing. Enabled several-fold increases in sales over this period. - Customer-collaborative product engineering. - Marketing, engineering sales support, and RFP preparation, with proven track record of winning bids. - In charge of government R&D grants, from proposal to final report. - Oversee Asian OEM and partners from R&D to mass-production deliverables. Customer Account Management - Expert in building customer relationships with depth of knowledge, trustworthiness and helpfulness. - Enabling customer success as a consultant and expert advisor; e.g. hosting industry workshops for decision makers and stakeholders, or presenting to city councils. Service Management - As interim Manager of Digital Services, transformed a small service department into a Network Operation Centre with advanced monitoring capabilities. - Developing and certifying network of subcontractor technicians across North America. - Resolving L3 escalations in engineering and customer management roles. Company Wide Roles - Transfer of available academic or theoretical knowledge to improve processes. - Driving the standardization/rationalization of VISIONiQ to reduce instructional, inventory and labour requirements. Realized substantial improvements in quality, turn-around time, and gross margins. - Dependable front-line leader for crisis resolution by application of technical depth and breadth, in the office or the field.

experiences.title: CTO and Co-founder
experiences.description: Designed and developed state-of-the-art modular LED display tile product from initial concepts and requirements to production and delivery all within tight budget constraints. Led technology/product development and managed technical team of 5 to develop full system spanning from modular waterproof electronics to web-based control application. Successfully brought the product to market with sales at price points of >$300K per unit. Strong working knowledge in multidisciplinary array of technical and non-technical areas including mechanical and electronics engineering, web software architecture, distributed embedded software, network engineering, imaging algorithms, business development, technical sales, supply-chain and production management

experiences.title: PhD candidate (Sensory Cybernetics Engineering Researcher)
experiences.description: While pursuing a PhD degree, developed the concept for a novel sensory augmentation device which employs operator eye-gaze to guide acoustic spatial filtering. Investigated across many disciplines including neurophysiology, cognitive psychology, eye-gaze estimation (EOG, corneal-reflection eye-tracking), control systems design, multi-sensor adaptive signal processing (beamforming), and low-power hardware/software implementation as a wearable device. Withdrew from PhD to pursue opportunity full-time with Luminautics.

experiences.title: Head Teaching Assistant
experiences.description: Led 2 other teaching assistants for 4th year ECE course in Sensory Communications to prepare/administer/grade tests, give lectures, and host tutorials and labs. Focused on the major design project aspect of the course, mentoring and advising over 40 teams in electronics, signal processing, programming, acoustics and experiment design.

experiences.title: Functional Verification Tester
experiences.description: Two summer internships. Analyzed test coverage and designed/executed test cases for IBM.com e-Commerce engine in collaboration with IBM BA's. Specialized as team expert on testing of Configurator web application.

education.activities_and_societies: Activities and Societies: OMGP Dragonboat team

Activities and Societies: Engineering Science Class Representative, New College Vanier Vanguards, Formula SAE team, Mechatronics Design Association, Engineering Business Club, Victoria College ViCrew dragonboat team

accomplishment_honors_awards.title: NSERC CREATE Academic Rehabilitation Engineering Scholarship (2 year)
Edward Rogers Sr. Department of ECE Fellowship
Autonomous Underwater Vehicle Competition "Best New Entry"
University of Toronto Engineering Entrance Scholarship

accomplishment_patents.title: SYSTEM AND METHODS FOR USER INSTALLATION OF MODULAR LED DISPLAYS

accomplishment_projects.title: Bioengineering Science Course Project: “Augmenting Closed Loop Insulin Controllers”
accomplishment_projects.description: Explored the feasibility of using a secondary source of feedback, the autonomic nervous system (ANS), to improve current closed-loop insulin controllers. Implemented an extensive physiological model of diabetic patients and added in ANS pathway. Designed controllers with/without ANS feedback to compare performance. Invited to present at IBBME Scientific Day 2008.

accomplishment_projects.title: 4th Year Course Design Project: “Automated Music Transcription”
accomplishment_projects.description: Designed and implemented a computerized system to transcribe acoustical music into written form. Featured functionality of rhythm detection, pitch extraction and instrument detection.

accomplishment_projects.title: Navigation System Leader for Autonomous Underwater Vehicle Competition
accomplishment_projects.description: Co-founded UofT Mechatronics Design Association and represented UofT in international competition against top schools such as MIT and Cornell. Won Best New Entry out of 5 new teams in the 9th annual AUVSI (Autonomous Unmanned Vehicle Systems International) Autonomous Underwater Vehicle competition in San Diego. Developed vehicle with passive sonar, magnetometer, pressure sensors, and underwater cameras with real-time algorithms. Spearheaded design of vehicle overall system architecture and control algorithms.

accomplishment_projects.title: Engineering Science Undergraduate Design Project
accomplishment_projects.description: Developed an automated inventory-stocking robot to working prototype under strict constraints. Took charge of project planning as team leader for a team of 3. Implemented control algorithms in microcontroller and developed all electrical systems, having no prior experience with either.
